# Sub PEQ filters
# miniDSP-style peaking filters plus normalized biquad coefficients
# Biquad convention: y[n] = b0*x[n] + b1*x[n-1] + b2*x[n-2] - a1*y[n-1] - a2*y[n-2]

Filter 1: ON PK Fc 58.389 Hz Gain -8.990 dB Q 6.6581
  biquad b0=0.999689673334 b1=-1.99902283209 b2=0.999347755719 a1=-1.99902283209 a2=0.999037429053
Filter 2: ON PK Fc 137.606 Hz Gain -5.400 dB Q 7.0458
  biquad b0=0.999596587857 b1=-1.99817623199 b2=0.998660686294 a1=-1.99817623199 a2=0.998257274152
Filter 3: ON PK Fc 102.431 Hz Gain +3.000 dB Q 3.4966
  biquad b0=1.0003324589 b1=-1.99834316114 b2=0.998055610438 a1=-1.99834316114 a2=0.998388069338
Filter 4: ON PK Fc 80.534 Hz Gain -6.598 dB Q 7.7813
  biquad b0=0.999736605185 b1=-1.99898235959 b2=0.999273523176 a1=-1.99898235959 a2=0.999010128361
Filter 5: ON PK Fc 44.068 Hz Gain +1.449 dB Q 3.8006
  biquad b0=1.00006337194 b1=-1.99929379446 b2=0.999238738454 a1=-1.99929379446 a2=0.99930211039
